Search Results for: insulin resistance
How Your Muscle Type Impacts Insulin Resistance
By
Ben Bikman
Your muscle isn’t all the same—and the type you have plays a major role in insulin sensitivity. Learn how slow- and fast-twitch fibers influence blood sugar control and what you can do to improve your metabolic health.
How Your Muscle Type Impacts Insulin Resistance Read More »
The Mouth–Metabolism Connection: How Your Gums Impact Insulin Resistance
By
Ben Bikman
Gum disease isn’t just a dental issue—it can directly impact your metabolism. Chronic inflammation from unhealthy gums can drive insulin resistance, disrupt blood sugar control, and even increase heart disease risk. Here’s why your oral health matters more than you think.
The Mouth–Metabolism Connection: How Your Gums Impact Insulin Resistance Read More »
NAD⁺, NADH, and Insulin Resistance: Why Metabolism Is a Battery Problem
By
Ben Bikman
Insulin resistance isn’t just a hormone or calorie problem—it’s a cellular energy problem. Inside your cells, a molecule called NAD⁺ acts like a rechargeable battery for metabolism. When excess glucose causes that battery to stay “overcharged,” energy flow slows, mitochondria struggle, and insulin signaling begins to break down.
NAD⁺, NADH, and Insulin Resistance: Why Metabolism Is a Battery Problem Read More »
Ben Bikman on Diary of a CEO: Insulin Resistance Explained
By
Ben Bikman
Ben Bikman joins the Diary of a CEO podcast to discuss insulin resistance, metabolism, and why insulin plays a central role in human health.
Ben Bikman on Diary of a CEO: Insulin Resistance Explained Read More »
How LPS From the Gut Drives Insulin Resistance
By
Ben Bikman
LPS-driven inflammation may be a hidden cause of insulin resistance and fatty liver. In this Metabolic Classroom lecture, Dr. Ben Bikman explains how gut permeability fuels metabolic disease — and what strengthens the gut barrier.
How LPS From the Gut Drives Insulin Resistance Read More »
Tirzepatide: Does It Really Work by Increasing Insulin?
By
Ben Bikman
Does tirzepatide really work by increasing insulin? Dr. Ben Bikman explains the science behind appetite suppression, insulin sensitivity, and metabolic health.
Tirzepatide: Does It Really Work by Increasing Insulin? Read More »
The Vagus Nerve: How Your Brain Controls Insulin and Metabolism
By
Ben Bikman
The vagus nerve acts like a communication highway between your brain, gut, liver, and pancreas. In this Metabolic Classroom lecture, Dr. Ben Bikman explains how this nerve helps regulate insulin, blood sugar, appetite, and how the body prepares for food before you even take a bite.
The Vagus Nerve: How Your Brain Controls Insulin and Metabolism Read More »
Is Alzheimer’s Disease a Metabolic Disorder?
By
Ben Bikman
For decades, Alzheimer’s disease was blamed on amyloid plaques in the brain. But a growing body of research suggests the disease may actually be driven by impaired brain metabolism and insulin resistance—sometimes called “Type 3 diabetes.”
Is Alzheimer’s Disease a Metabolic Disorder? Read More »
Heart Health: The Markers That Actually Matter
By
Ben Bikman
Nearly half of heart attack patients have normal LDL cholesterol. Research shows insulin resistance and the triglyceride-to-HDL ratio may be stronger predictors of cardiovascular risk.
Heart Health: The Markers That Actually Matter Read More »
Male Menopause & Metabolism
By
Ben Bikman
Men experience a real hormonal decline called andropause—but unlike female menopause, much of it is driven by metabolism, not age. Here’s how testosterone changes, why insulin resistance and body fat accelerate the decline, and what men can do to support healthy hormonal function.
Male Menopause & Metabolism Read More »
